verses.commentary: Reached Beersheba. Offered sacrifices to God of Isaac. Journey begins. Beersheba, southern border of promised land. Stops to worship. God of his father Isaac.
God spoke in vision at night. Jacob! Jacob! Here I am (hineni, הִנֵּנִי). Divine encounter. Double name call (like Abraham, 22:11). Jacob responds. Ready to listen.
I am God, the God of your father, he said. I am God, God of your father. God identifies himself. Leaving promised land feels wrong. But God commands it.
God's presence promised. Return guaranteed (not Jacob personally, but his descendants).
Jacob brought with him to Egypt his sons and grandsons and his daughters and granddaughters, all his offspring. All offspring.
Levi's sons. Three sons. Levitical lines.
Judah's sons. Er and Onan died in Canaan (chapter 38). Perez's sons.
Besides Dinah. Thirty-three total. Leah's line counted. Daughter mentioned.
Asher's sons. Four sons. Beriah's sons.
By Asenath. Born in Egypt before family arrived.
Direct descendants. Not counting wives. Sixty-six persons. Core family count.
Two sons born to Joseph in Egypt. Jacob's family in Egypt: seventy total. Add Joseph, Manasseh, Ephraim, and Jacob himself. Acts 7:14 says seventy-five including grandchildren.
Uses title Israel. Emotional reunion. Long weeping.
Jacob's response. Life complete. Simeon's words fulfilled (Luke 2:29-30). Seen enough.
